-{"id":"gt-9uy0","title":"Remove 'spawn' terminology from molecular chemistry","description":"'Spawn' should ONLY be used for polecats (workers). Molecules use different verbs:
- pour: create mol from proto (persistent)
- wisp: create wisp from proto (ephemeral)
- run: execute molecule
- bond: attach molecule to something

Found ~100+ instances of 'bd mol spawn' or 'spawn' used with molecules.

**Correct terminology:**
- gt spawn = spawn polecat (OK)
- bd pour <proto> = create persistent molecule
- bd wisp <proto> = create ephemeral wisp
- bd run <proto> = create and execute
- bd mol bond = attach molecule

**Docs needing updates:**
- prompts/roles/deacon.md: 'bd mol spawn' → 'bd wisp'
- docs/molecular-chemistry.md: 'Spawn Mol/Wisp' → 'Pour/Wisp'
- docs/molecule-algebra.md: 'Spawn as Mol when...'
- docs/sling-design.md, architecture.md, vision.md

**Code needing updates:**
- internal/cmd/prime.go: 'bd mol spawn' → 'bd wisp' (~6 instances)
- internal/cmd/sling.go: rename spawnMoleculeFromProto → pourMoleculeFromProto

P1 because docs show non-existent commands.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:06:43.805547-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:12:43.772336-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9uy0","depends_on_id":"gt-jo9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:06:49.341648-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-9uy0","title":"Remove 'spawn' terminology from molecular chemistry","description":"Clean all docs and code to present ONLY current terminology. No migration paths, no "old vs new" tables, no deprecated flags. The codebase should read as if the current design was always the design.

**Terminology rules:**
- spawn = polecats/workers ONLY
- pour = create persistent mol
- wisp = create ephemeral wisp  
- run = create and execute

**Scope:**
- Remove all "Old → New" migration tables
- Remove deprecated flag documentation (--persistent, etc.)
- Remove chemistry-design-changes.md entirely (it is a migration doc)
- Clean all docs to use current terminology only
- Rename spawn functions in code that deal with molecules","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:06:43.805547-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:51:45.118248-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9uy0","depends_on_id":"gt-jo9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:06:49.341648-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-9wv0","title":"gt spawn should verify daemon is running for polecat triggering","description":"gt spawn notifies the Deacon (via mail) that a polecat was started, expecting the Deacon to trigger it once Claude is ready. But if the daemon isn't running, the mail sits unread and the polecat never gets triggered.

## Current Behavior
1. gt spawn starts polecat session
2. gt spawn sends POLECAT_STARTED to deacon/
3. (assumes daemon will trigger polecat)

## Problem
If gt daemon isn't running, step 3 never happens and polecat sits at prompt.

## Solution
In gt spawn, after session start:
1. Check if daemon is running (gt daemon status)
2. If not running, either:
   a. Start daemon: gt daemon start
   b. Or warn user: 'Daemon not running, polecat may not auto-start'

## Alternative
The user can manually trigger with gt nudge, but automated flow should work.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:03.71521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:03.71521-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9wv0","depends_on_id":"gt-bjft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:12.187224-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-9za0","title":"Mol Mall: Molecule marketplace distribution","description":"Design the Mol Mall distribution mechanism for sharing molecules.

## Core Insight

Mol Mall is just a registry of molecules.jsonl fragments. No special format needed.

## Distribution Model

```
Mol Mall (registry)
    │
    │ bd mol search "security"
    ▼
Molecule catalog (searchable index)
    │
    │ bd mol install mol-security-scan
    ▼
Download molecules.jsonl fragment
    │
    │ Append to ~/.beads/molecules.jsonl
    ▼
Available locally: bd mol list
```

## Registry Format

Simple JSON index served from HTTPS:

```json
{
  "molecules": [
    {
      "id": "mol-security-scan",
      "title": "Security Scan",
      "description": "OWASP Top 10 vulnerability checker",
      "labels": ["plugin", "code-review", "tier:sonnet"],
      "author": "anthropic",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "url": "https://mol-mall.anthropic.com/molecules/mol-security-scan.jsonl"
    }
  ]
}
```

## CLI Commands

```bash
bd mol search <query>           # Search registry
bd mol info <id>                # Show molecule details
bd mol install <id>             # Download and install
bd mol uninstall <id>           # Remove from catalog
bd mol update                   # Update all installed molecules
```

## Installation Locations

```
~/.beads/molecules.jsonl        # User-level (default install location)
~/gt/.beads/molecules.jsonl     # Town-level (shared across rigs)
.beads/molecules.jsonl          # Project-level (team-specific)
```

## Version Management

Molecules can have versions via labels: `version:1.0.0`
Install specific version: `bd mol install mol-security-scan@1.0.0`

## Auth (Future)

Private registries could require auth token.
Enterprise Mol Malls for internal distribution.

## Related

- gt-u818: Plugin System (plugins ARE molecules)
- molecular-chemistry.md: Format documentation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T05:14:35.698369-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T05:14:35.698369-08:00"}

+{"id":"gt-i6k1","title":"Clean up duplicate patrol protos (gt-qflq, gt-iep9)","description":"There are duplicate protos for patrol molecules:

## Duplicates

| Old Proto | New Proto | Name |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| gt-qflq | mol-witness-patrol | mol-witness-patrol |
| gt-iep9 | mol-deacon-patrol | mol-deacon-patrol |

The old gt-* prefix protos were created before the formula cooking system.
The new mol-* prefix protos were created by `bd cook`.

## Action

1. Close or delete the old gt-* protos
2. Update any references to use the new mol-* protos
3. Verify `bd mol list` shows clean output

## Root Cause

The bd cook command uses the formula name as the proto ID (mol-*), 
not the project prefix (gt-*). This is probably correct behavior,
but means we have legacy protos to clean up.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:50:11.424341-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:50:11.424341-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-j6s8","title":"Refinery startup: bond mol-refinery-patrol on start","description":"Wire up Refinery to automatically bond its patrol molecule on startup.

## Current state
- mol-refinery-patrol exists in builtin_molecules.go
- prompts/roles/refinery.md describes the protocol
- Refinery doesn't auto-bond on startup

## Desired behavior
On Refinery session start:
1. gt prime detects RoleRefinery
2. Check for existing in-progress patrol: bd list --status=in_progress --assignee=refinery
3. If found: resume from current step
4. If not found: bd mol bond mol-refinery-patrol --wisp
5. Output patrol context to agent

## Implementation options
A) Add to gt prime (outputRefineryPatrolContext)
B) Add startup hook in refinery CLAUDE.md
C) Both (prime detects, template reinforces)

## Testing
- Start refinery session
- Verify patrol bonds automatically
- Kill mid-patrol, restart, verify resumes

## Depends on
- gt-3x0z.10 (existing issue for Refinery patrol)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/dementus","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:34.739741-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:16:16.780172-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:16:16.780172-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main"}
+{"id":"gt-j755","title":"mol-polecat-arm: Add nudge_text variable definition","description":"The mol-polecat-arm formula references `{{nudge_text}}` in the execute step but doesn't define it in the variables section.

## Current variables section

```yaml
variables:
  - name: polecat_name
    required: true
  - name: rig
    required: true
```

## Missing

```yaml
  - name: nudge_text
    required: false
    default: "How's progress? Need any help?"
    description: Text to send when nudging the polecat
```

## Usage in formula

```bash
tmux send-keys -t gt-{{rig}}-{{polecat_name}} "{{nudge_text}}" Enter
```

## Fix

Add nudge_text to the variables section with a sensible default.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:51:29.644259-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:51:29.644259-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-j87","title":"Design: Work flow simulation and validation","description":"Validate GGT designs through simulation before implementation.

## Validation Approaches

### 1. Dry-Run Simulation (Recommended First)
Mayor walks through scenarios mentally/on paper:
- "If polecat Toast signals done with dirty git state, what happens?"
- "If Witness context fills mid-verification, what state is lost?"
- "If two polecats try to close same issue, what happens?"

Create beads for any gaps discovered.

### 2. Real Work in gastown-py
Use Python Gas Town to stress-test assumptions:
- Run actual batch work on test repos
- Observe edge cases in practice
- Document issues found

### 3. Edge Case Analysis
Systematic review of failure modes:
- Agent crashes mid-operation
- Network failures during sync
- Concurrent access to shared state
- Context limits hit at bad times

## Key Scenarios to Validate

- [ ] Witness session cycling (state preservation)
- [ ] Polecat decommission with dirty state
- [ ] Merge conflicts in queue
- [ ] Beads sync conflicts between workers
- [ ] Escalation path (stuck worker -> Mayor)
- [ ] Cross-rig communication
- [ ] Federation mail routing (future)

## Success Criteria

- No data loss scenarios identified
- Clear recovery paths for all failure modes
- Edge cases either handled or documented as limitations
- Design improves as model cognition improves

## Output

For each scenario validated:
1. Document in relevant bead if issue found
2. Create new beads for missing functionality
3. Update architecture.md if design changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:24:11.251841-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:49.858717-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-wusk","title":"Layered context onboarding pattern","description":"Pattern from handoff discussion:

## Pattern: Layered Context Onboarding

Town CLAUDE.md (user/org) -> Rig CLAUDE.md (project) -> Role priming

## Ultra-compressed HOP for workers (no reveal)

- Permanent record: All work tracked. Outcomes matter.
- Quality gates: Molecule steps exist for a reason.
- Attribution: Completions build your track record.
- Handoff clean: Leave state any worker can continue.

## Recommendation

Create Town @AGENTS.md for shared worker context that all workers see.
This provides common behavioral guidance without revealing full HOP context.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:55:11.984103-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:55:11.984103-08:00"}

+{"id":"gt-x0a5","title":"Patrol formulas: Inconsistent variable placeholder syntax","description":"The patrol formulas use inconsistent syntax for variable placeholders:

## Inconsistency

| Formula | Syntax | Examples |
|---------|--------|----------|
| mol-polecat-arm | `{{var}}` | `{{polecat_name}}`, `{{rig}}` |
| mol-witness-patrol | `<var>` | `<rig>`, `<handoff-bead-id>` |
| mol-refinery-patrol | `<var>` | `<polecat-branch>` |

## Expected

All formulas should use consistent syntax, probably `{{var}}` since that's what
the variables section defines.

## Fix

Update mol-witness-patrol and mol-refinery-patrol to use `{{var}}` syntax
where appropriate, or document that `<var>` is for human-filled placeholders
vs `{{var}}` for cook-time interpolation.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:51:48.126618-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:51:48.126618-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-x7c","title":"Work assignment mail not received by polecat","description":"When gt spawn sends a work assignment:

1. gt spawn says '✓ Work assignment sent'
2. Polecat runs 'gt mail inbox' 
3. Shows '0 messages, 0 unread'

The work assignment mail never arrived at the polecat's inbox.

## Observed in session
- Spawned polecat dementus on gt-th7
- Polecat checked inbox: empty
- Polecat couldn't find issue (separate sync bug)

## Possible causes
- Mail routing issue for polecat addresses
- gt spawn not actually sending mail
- Mail sent to wrong address format","notes":"This was likely caused by stale beads in old polecats. With gt-9nf (fresh polecats), polecats now use shared rig beads via redirect file, eliminating sync issues. Mail routing uses town beads correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:18:12.39878-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:16:00.862028-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T10:16:00.862028-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed by gt-9nf: fresh polecats use shared beads, mail routes correctly to town beads"}
